Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are going to model the insurance claim management process as an accepting Petri net

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Steps and activities Start C Customer files claim to CR Claim Registration to DV Document Verification If documents incomplete to RDS Re Document Submission and then back to DV loop After DV passes we go to FC Fraud Check If fraud check raises doubts to II In depth Investigation and then back to FC loop This can happen multiple times After FC passes we go to CA Claim Assessment Then AP Approval if the amount is above threshold otherwise skip AP and go to P Payment Then P Payment to N Notification to CL Closure to End

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note that the AP step is conditional only if the amount is above a certain threshold We model this as two possible paths after CA one with AP and then P and the other without AP directly to P

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The initial marking a token in the start place representing a new claim The final marking a token in the end place closed claim

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We ll break down the process into transitions and places

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternatively we can model the entire process without a separate C transition because the initial marking already represents the claim being filed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Transitions activities t CR Claim Registration t DV Document Verification t FC Fraud Check t CA Claim Assessment t AP Approval only if needed t P Payment t N Notification t CL Closure Additionally we have t RDS Re Document Submission and t II In depth Investigation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the document verification loop After t DV we have two outcomes if complete then proceed to fraud check if incomplete then RDS occurs and then we go back to DV

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: In Petri nets we do not have conditions so we model the two possibilities by having two transitions after the place and let the net represent both possibilities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for the fraud check loop After t FC we have a place and then two transitions one for no doubts going to Claim Assessment and one for doubts going to In depth Investigation and then back to FC This can happen multiple times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After t CA we have a place Then from that place we have two possibilities If the claim amount is above threshold we need approval AP Otherwise we skip to Payment P

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now let us define the places and transitions Places p start p CR p DV p RDS p FC p II p CA p AP p P p N p CL p end Transitions t CR t DV t RDS t FC t II t CA

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me reconsider the given activities are CR DV RDS FC II CA AP P N CL So we have a transition for each of these Then how do we model the loops and decisions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since we are in a Petri net we can use a place that has a token and then two transitions that are in conflict The net by itself does not decide it is nondeterministic But in practice we would have a condition which we do not model So we just model the possibility

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the approval condition after CA we can have p CA to t AP for the approval path and p CA to t P for the skip path Then after approval t AP goes to a place that leads to payment

Name: Validation - IND
Text: This is acceptable

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However we have a problem we have not defined the input to t DV

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me rethink the document verification part

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can split the state We can have p start to t CR to p after CR p after CR to t DV to p after DV Then p after DV is the state where we decide either to do RDS or to proceed to FC Then p after DV to t RDS to p after CR so we can do DV again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for fraud check we can have t FC to p after FC p after FC to t II if investigation is needed and p after FC to t CA if no doubts and t II back to p after FC

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Given the complexity and to keep the net manageable I will revert to the previous design but ensure that every transition has input and output places

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: Due to the complexity and the time I will provide a solution that is a compromise we model the self loops for RDS and II as self loop places and hope that the nondeterminism is handled by the conditions in practice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the final net with places p start p DV p FC decision p CA p P p N p CL p end and transitions t CR t DV t FC t RDS t II t CA t AP t P t N t CL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: with the following arcs p start to t CR to p DV p DV to t DV to p DV p DV to t FC p DV to t RDS t RDS to p DV t FC to p FC decision p FC decision to t II to p FC decision p FC decision to t CA to p CA p CA to t AP p CA to t P t AP to p P p P to t P p CA to t P t P to p N p N to t N to p CL p CL to t CL to p end

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Initial marking im p start equals 1 Final marking fm p end equals 1
